title: Go-to-Market Strategy
shortDescription: Build a comprehensive go-to-market plan covering positioning, channels, timeline, enablement, and success metrics.
jobsToBeDone: Plan a coordinated market entry or product launch
Align marketing, sales, and product on launch strategy
Identify the highest-leverage channels for target audience

inputs.label: Product Description
inputs.example: DataSync — a real-time data integration platform that connects SaaS tools (CRM, support, billing) into a unified customer data layer, with no-code connectors and sub-second sync

inputs.label: Target Audience
inputs.example: RevOps and data teams at mid-market B2B SaaS companies (100-1000 employees) who currently use manual CSV exports or fragile Zapier chains to move data between tools

inputs.label: Positioning Statement
inputs.example: Unlike Segment (too expensive) or Fivetran (too technical), DataSync gives RevOps teams real-time data unification without writing code or managing infrastructure

inputs.label: Launch Context
inputs.example: V1 launch — we have 12 beta customers with strong NPS (72), $0 marketing spend so far, 3-person team. Goal is first 100 paying customers in 90 days

inputs.label: Competitive Landscape
inputs.example: Segment (enterprise, expensive), Fivetran (ETL-focused, technical), Zapier (widespread but unreliable for real-time), Census (reverse ETL only). No dominant player in the 'RevOps-first' positioning

inputs.label: Budget & Resource Constraints
inputs.example: $15K marketing budget, no dedicated marketing hire, founders doing sales. Can invest engineering time in product-led growth features
